shots.narration: In 1950, Dr. Abraham Jacob Weintraub left France for Sri Lanka and India with the intention of staying only two months to meet with spiritual masters, in particular with Ramana Maharishi

Following this meeting, he decided to follow the holy woman and later became a monk or Swami

in Ma's organization, taking the name of Swami Vijayananda, the victory of bliss.

Swami Vijayananda never returned to France and spent nearly 60 years in India, including 17 years as a hermit in the mountains of the Himalayas. This gentil grand-père or sweet grandfather, as some French followers like to call him, welcomed Westerners to Ma's ashram in Kankhal,
as some French followers like to call him, welcomed Westerners to Ma's ashram in Kankhal, where the tomb of Anandamai Ma is located until his death on April 5, 2010 at the age of 95.
where the tomb of Anandamai Ma is located until his death on April 5, 2010 at the age of 95.

Finally, when you realize that you are neither dreaming nor are you seeing an illusion, you will understand you have entered a new dimension of reality. This is how the German novelist, Melita Marshma, described her first encounter with Anandamai Ma in her book, Encountering Bliss, My Journey Through India with Anandamai Ma. Ma Anandamai is considered a true giant in Indian spirituality, who is revered by people in the East and the West alike, as one among the great saints of modern India, along with saints like Ramakrishna, Vivekananda, Aurobindu Ghosh, Ramana Maharishi and Paramahansa Yogananda.
Aurobindu Ghosh, Ramana Maharishi and Paramahansa Yogananda. Anandamai Ma literally means bliss-permeated mother.
Anandamai Ma literally means bliss-permeated mother. It was as early as 1920s that people close to her
It was as early as 1920s that people close to her began to realize the essence of Mother Divine in her

began to realize the essence of Mother Divine in her and started calling her Mataji or often just Ma. It was one of her early disciples called Bhaiyaji who gave her the title of Ma Anandamai, meaning Mother Full of Bliss. Soon people all over the country and from different quarters of the world who flocked to her for spiritual illumination started calling her Anandamai Ma or bliss-permeated mother. On April 30, 1896, in a remote rural village of Kyora in East Bengal,
in a remote rural village of Kyora in East Bengal, a girl-child was born to a poor but devout

a girl-child was born to a poor but devout Vaishnava Brahmin couple. They named her Nirmala Sundari, meaning Immaculate Beauty or Taintless Beauty. According to Professor Bithika Mukherjee, the principal biographer of Anandamai Ma, she was a very detached child right from her early years,
she was a very detached child right from her early years,
showing little interest in her surrounding environment.
People often thought she was intellectually challenged. But this detachment did not mean any withdrawal from life.
Nirmala Sundari was indeed a very happy child.

She communed with animals and trees like St. Francis of Assisi and often fell into spiritual trances. At the age of 13, Nirmala Sundari was married to a much older person, Ramani Mohan Chakravarti. He later became her disciple and she called him Bholenath, one of the names of Lord Shiva. Ma went through her marriage life with complete commitment,
Ma went through her marriage life with complete commitment,
treating her husband like a devoted wife, but she never consummated her marriage with sexual union or procreation.
but she never consummated her marriage with sexual union or procreation. It is said that whenever the thoughts of physical attraction and lust occurred to her husband,
It is said that whenever the thoughts of physical attraction and lust occurred to her husband, Ma's body would assume the terrible and awe-inspiring form of the goddess Kali
Ma's body would assume the terrible and awe-inspiring form of the goddess Kali
Ma's body would assume the terrible and awe-inspiring form of the goddess Kali

Ma's body would assume the terrible and awe-inspiring form of the goddess Kali and the poor husband would retreat in submission and guilt. Bholenath was troubled and frustrated at first, but was gradually convinced that his wife was in fact a manifestation of the Divine Mother.
but was gradually convinced that his wife was in fact a manifestation of the Divine Mother. He later took spiritual initiation from his wife and began to relate to her as his guru.

He later took spiritual initiation from his wife and began to relate to her as his guru. In a remarkable reversal from the perspective of Dharma Shastra or the Hindu code, he spent the rest of his life as his wife's disciple,
he spent the rest of his life as his wife's disciple, caring for her physical well-being as she seemed completely uninterested in her body

caring for her physical well-being as she seemed completely uninterested in her body and mediating between her and her growing number of devotees. Ma, in fact, distanced herself from the traditional role of a Hindu married woman

Ma, in fact, distanced herself from the traditional role of a Hindu married woman and broke the ideal of the perfect Hindu woman or Pativrata by her refusal to toe the line. Karen Pekelis, who undertook an interesting study on the female gurus in Asia, considers this refusal of the socially defined role of a woman
considers this refusal of the socially defined role of a woman as a key instrumental element in conferring the charismatic status

as a key instrumental element in conferring the charismatic status Ma Anandamai held among other female saints and gurus. In 1918, at the age of 22, Ma moved with her husband to Bajitpur in eastern Bengal.

In 1918, at the age of 22, Ma moved with her husband to Bajitpur in eastern Bengal. There she initiated an intense and prolonged phase of spiritual self-discipline or sadhana. Unlike other sages and yogic masters, she never received any direction or lessons from
Unlike other sages and yogic masters, she never received any direction or lessons from a guru. She taught herself and was able to perform the varied yogic postures, asanas and symbolic
She taught herself and was able to perform the varied yogic postures, asanas and symbolic
She taught herself and was able to perform the varied yogic postures, asanas and symbolic
She taught herself and was able to perform the varied yogic postures, asanas and symbolic and ritualistic gestures, mudras, all by herself, spontaneously.
and ritualistic gestures, mudras, all by herself, spontaneously.

and ritualistic gestures, mudras, all by herself, spontaneously. Ma called this phase sadhana leela. Leela means play or game. For Ma, there was nothing to be attained spiritually through sadhana, although she learned, practiced and perfected it without any masters. Her state had always been one of spiritual realization, according to Ma. Anandamai Ma continued her sadhana or self-disciplining and that eventually led into her initiation. On August 3rd, 1922, Ma conducted her own initiation, simultaneously becoming the disciple, Shishya,

On August 3rd, 1922, Ma conducted her own initiation, simultaneously becoming the disciple, Shishya, the master, guru and the divinity, Ishtha. In his book, Autobiography of a Yogi, Yogananda describes how Ma responded when asked to tell something about herself. There is so little to tell, Father. Before I came on this earth, Father, I was the same.

There is so little to tell, Father. Before I came on this earth, Father, I was the same. As a little girl, I was the same. I grew into womanhood, but still I was the same. Fully convinced of Ma's divinity, Yogananda remarked that Anandamai Ma was always one with the Eternal. This is the foremost, nay the only, duty of man. We may try to summarize the teachings of Anandamai Ma as focusing on two central themes, on self-realization and on action.

Anandam or bliss is a highly philosophical idea in Indian tradition. God or the Absolute is described as Sachitananda, a fusion of existence, consciousness and bliss. Thus, in Indian philosophical tradition, bliss is not just joy or happiness. It is the ultimate manifestation of God himself. This is the reason why Anandamai Ma, the bliss permeated mother, was considered to be the manifestation of the Divine Mother by her disciples.

This is the reason why Anandamai Ma, the bliss permeated mother, was considered to be the manifestation of the Divine Mother by her disciples. The bliss, Ma propagated and emanated, was the higher state of God.

The bliss, Ma propagated and emanated, was the higher state of God. self-realization attainable by everyone through proper sadhana and proper living. The life of the blissful mother is an eloquent testimony to the abiding strength of Indian spirituality. Ma Anandamai's message is particularly appropriate in a world where the notion of progress is no longer taken from gospel truth and the whole array of our modern values

notion of progress is no longer taken from gospel truth and the whole array of our modern values is undergoing an agonizing reappraisal. Anandamai Ma's diagnosis the present disease of civilization as over-secularization. She suggests that the cure from our severe illness cannot be bought about by engineers, sociologists and psychologists but by doctors of the soul. She prescribed a drastic revolution in our views and attitudes from outwardness to inwardness, from materialism to spirituality and from man-centeredness to God-centeredness, observes Alexander Lipsky. of the Divine Mother. She continues to teach and guide thousands of her followers even today. The supreme lesson of bliss she taught, by example, lives on.

Ma's presence is external in this world. In her own words, bliss, one who passes away, in fact, merges into the one who is ever-existent. Anandamai Ma, the mother of bliss, is indeed ever-existent.
